title: abies douglasii var. macrocarpa
intro: Abies douglasii var. macrocarpa, also known as the Sierra fir, is a variety of the Douglas fir tree that is native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The medicinal properties of the Sierra fir include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of the plant include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function, as well as its ability to act as a natural expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. Herbal preparations of the Sierra fir can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
